title: Copy Boards and Folders
excerpt:
Our engineering team is new to Monday.com. I have the following suggestions: 


Copy a folder and all of its boards and contents. E.g. I have a ‘Template Folder’ with standardised boards, pulses & columns. I want to star…

posts.author: Njohnston (Noel Johnston)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our engineering team is new to Monday.com. I have the following suggestions:

  1. Copy a folder and all of its boards and contents. E.g. I have a ‘Template Folder’ with standardised boards, pulses & columns. I want to start a new job, but there is no way to copy the template folder and its contents when you right click. instead, I had to duplicate each board, then ‘move board to folder’ ‘job1’. Then I had to remove the ‘Duplicate of’ from each board name, which was time consuming

  2. Same goes for Groups. I had to ‘Duplicate group’, then ‘move group to board’. Because I have 2 job folders, with the same board name, I was not sure which board to move it too:

  3. It would be useful to shift-select many boards at once, and perform cut, copy, paste to other folders or the root. The copy command is the same as ‘duplicate, move’ but one less step.

  4. When importing a list from excel from my list of tasks, I was unable to select groupings for each task. For example, say I have 100 tasks, divided into 10 items per 10 groups. When you import, all 100 items appear sequentially. It was time consuming to then manually create each group in Monday.com, then multiple-select and ‘move to group’.

thanks

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 on this one feature. hope it will be avaliable soon.

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yea this was a pain point for us too when we started some of our bigger boards. I ended up making an additional column with the Group Title I wanted and set it up as a Status Column during import. It was easier to do a sort and multi-select and move to a new group. Not perfect but a decent work aroudn if you haven’t found it yet for the interim.

Njohnston:

When importing a list from excel from my list of tasks, I was unable to select groupings for each task. For example, say I have 100 tasks, divided into 10 items per 10 groups. When you import, all 100 items appear sequentially. It was time consuming to then manually create each group in Monday.com, then multiple-select and ‘move to group’.

posts.author: tomsvision (Tom Guy)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1.

For each new project, I need 3 boards. So I created a folder with those 3 boards in it. The 3 boards have linked / mirrored items in them. I want to be able to duplicate the folder and keep the boards linked to each other.

Tom

posts.author: KyleDanielle (Kyle Seem)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 for sure!

This would be amazingly helpful! We also have template folders with several templated boards that need to be recreated many times. This would be a huge time saver and help reduce user error/oversight.

posts.author: LGal (Lynn )
posts.cookedHtml: This would definitely be helpful for us as well. We have multiple template boards for client projects and the ability to copy the folder as opposed to individual boards would save a lot of time and effort. I’m surprised this isn’t a common need and wonder if others have found a workaround.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @LGal - This can be done with a little monday․com developer workaround called a Workspace Template app. Using this approach, you can copy any number of linked template boards and dashboards with one click. I’d be happy to give you a personal tour of how it works. You can book a time with us here: Book a call with Polished Geek: Do more with monday․com

posts.author: Bibi (Bibi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are there any news about this topic?
My team/ project managers really need this feature provided. The boards and dashboards for every new project are of the same structure and there is a folder with templates of these. With the current (limited) possibilities they have to do all the steps mentioned above for the folder, and each board and dashboard in it: create + rename folder, copy/duplicate + rename + move each board and dashboard.
A reaction by the Monday team would be really appreciated. @monday-team Thank you so much.

posts.author: kathieryn (Kathryn Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

We would love to see the ability to duplicate folders as well. We are just getting set up on Monday, but for each project folder we have 3 boards that are going to be linked together, so it would be amazing to be able to copy those over to a new project without breaking the links. Thank you!

posts.author: Ozi (Oz Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

Spending hours on duplicating and moving boards…

Thanks

posts.author: ChayceA (Chayce)
posts.cookedHtml: I couldn’t agree more with this post. This is one of those nuances that could strongly improve Monday. When one spends hours recreating the same board or folder with multiple boards, it really takes away from our productivity.

posts.author: bluewater2222 (Todd Bluewater)
posts.cookedHtml:
Found the way to accomplish this. I have about 15 folders with 30 or so boards that make up my ‘Client Folder’… I stumbled across the Cross Account Copier (Admin Section). It allows you to copy folders from one account to another, and sets up new ID numbers for the folders, boards, etc. The boards have to be of the Main Type, and some links need to be fixed - errors show exactly which links got broke. Of the 15 folders, and 30 boards, there were only 4 links that needed updating.

To make it work, just copy the folder to the SAME account you are already using… and Presto!

…Keep in mind you cannot choose the destination - the copy goes in your top level workspace. From there, just move the folder to the right workspace and you’re good to go…

posts.author: janelegall (Jane)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this one? It’s a major pain point of our organisation too.

posts.author: christinap (Christina Punchard)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, Thank you for this workaround however, I’ve found that it won’t allow me to do it as it says it’s “only available to collaborators”. Has there been further developments that will allow me to recreate a folder of boards yet do you know?

posts.author: hobbit (wayne giles)
posts.cookedHtml: we have the need to duplicate multiple boards or a folder with set boards within them in one go rather than duplicate 1 board at a time, this would save people so much time.

posts.author: Audrey (A)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
It is indeed a pain point
@monday.com why such request has not been developed from 2019?
The feature is available per board so it should be possible to have the option of select a group of boards (ie a folder) and duplicate.
Could you confirm that this feature can be quickly developed and released ?
Thanks a lot

posts.author: arun.paudyal (Arun Paudyal [Decadis AG])
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear community,

Arun here from Decadis AG – monday marketplace partner. We’ve listened closely to the challenges faced by customers like you, and built a solution that’s truly a game-changer. Meet Batch Hero for monday, your superhero for bulk operations and board management.

Looking at the feature request, I believe our app partially accomplishes it. Batch Hero is capable of individual or batch copy of boards into desired workspace or folder. As of now, we do not support copying of folders, but that’s a nice request and we will keep it under our radar for further investigation.


(For fullscreen view, right click on gif and select “Open Image in New Tab”)

And the best part? The app is still free!

Give it a try and see it for yourself. If you have further questions or would like to discuss your use cases, kindly reach out to us.

Best,
Arun
